title: AWS three-stage evolution path for n8n
mermaid:
flowchart LR
    A["Stage 1: Lightsail or EC2 single-server"] --> B["Stage 2: EC2 + RDS + Secrets Manager + ALB + CloudWatch"]
    B --> C["Stage 3: ECS/Fargate + RDS + Secrets Manager + ALB + CloudWatch Container Insights"]
    A1["Docker Compose / local Postgres / simple domain"] --> A
    B1["Managed DB, separated secrets, load balancer, logs"] --> B
    C1["Task definitions, services, target groups, autoscaling, container logs"] --> C

stages.name: Stage 1: Lightsail or EC2 single-server
stages.bestFor: AWS account users who still need a VPS-shaped deployment with low architectural overhead
buildingBlocks: Lightsail container service or EC2 instance
EBS or instance storage backup plan
assemblyAndOpsCost: Patch operating system and Docker runtime
Monitor logs manually or add CloudWatch
moveForwardWhen: Database backup and restore need managed service support
Secrets need managed storage and rotation
Single instance failure is no longer acceptable

stages.name: Stage 2: EC2 + RDS + Secrets Manager + ALB + CloudWatch
stages.bestFor: Important n8n production workloads that need managed database, separated secrets, ingress health checks, and centralized logs
advantages: Database durability improves through RDS backups and restore options
Secrets are not stored in instance files
ALB gives health checks and TLS/HTTP routing
CloudWatch creates a central operations surface
assemblyAndOpsCost: ALB listener and target group health check design
RDS backup window and retention management
Secrets rotation and application reload behavior
CloudWatch log retention and alert tuning
moveForwardWhen: Deployment frequency and rollback needs outgrow hand-managed EC2
Task-level scaling and immutable revisions become important

stages.name: Stage 3: ECS/Fargate + RDS + Secrets Manager + ALB + CloudWatch Container Insights
stages.bestFor: Teams that need container orchestration, task isolation, service autoscaling, and production release pipelines
advantages: No EC2 host patching for Fargate tasks
ALB target groups and ECS services support rolling replacement
Container logs and metrics are first-class operations inputs
assemblyAndOpsCost: awsvpc networking and security group mapping
Execution role and task role separation
DB connection limits and worker concurrency control
CloudWatch logs and Container Insights cost control
moveForwardWhen: Queue mode and worker separation are required
Multiple n8n instances need coordinated deployments
Compliance or governance requires formal IAM and audit controls

acceptanceConclusion.awsPowerSource: AWS is powerful because it exposes compute, networking, database, secrets, load balancing, logging, metrics, IAM, backup, and container orchestration as composable building blocks.
acceptanceConclusion.costOfPower: Each building block adds IAM permissions, network rules, health checks, billing dimensions, alarms, backups, failure modes, and documentation responsibilities.
